We consider the addition of two 2's complement numbers a_{n-1}a_{n-2}...a_0 and b_{n-1}b_{n-2}...b_0. A binary adder for adding unsigned binary numbers is used to add the two numbers. The sum is denoted by s_{n-1}s_{n-2}...s_0 and the carry-out by C_out. Which one of the following options correctly identifies the overflow condition?

options.text: C_out XOR a_{n-1} XOR b_{n-1} XOR s_{n-1}
a_{n-1} XOR b_{n-1} XOR s_{n-1} XOR C_out

sourceQuestion:
Consider numbers represented in 4-bit Gray code. Let h = g_3 g_2 g_1 g_0 be the Gray code representation of a number n, and let g_3' g_2' g_1' g_0' be the Gray code of (n+1) value of the number. Which one of the following functions is correct?

A. g_0(h) = sum(1, 2, 3, 10, 11, 13, 14, 15)
B. g_0(h) = sum(4, 9, 10, 12, 13, 15)
C. g_0(h) = sum(2, 4, 5, 6, 9, 12, 13, 15)
D. g_0(h) = sum(1, 6, 7, 10, 11, 13, 14, 15)

sourceQuestion:
When two 4-bit numbers A = a_3 a_2 a_1 a_0 and B = b_3 b_2 b_1 b_0 in 2's complement representation (with a_3 and b_3 as the most significant bits) are added using a ripple-carry adder, the sum bits obtained are s_3 s_2 s_1 s_0 and the carry bits are c_4 c_3 c_2 c_1. An overflow is said to have occurred if

A. the carry bit c_4 is 1
B. all the carry bits c_4 c_3 c_2 c_1 are 1
C. (a_3 XOR b_3) is 1
D. (a_3 XOR b_3 XOR s_3) is 1
sourceAnswer: D. (a_3 XOR b_3 XOR s_3) is 1

parsed.stem: When two 4-bit numbers A = a_3 a_2 a_1 a_0 and B = b_3 b_2 b_1 b_0 in 2's complement representation (with a_3 and b_3 as the most significant bits) are added using a ripple-carry adder, the sum bits obtained are s_3 s_2 s_1 s_0 and the carry bits are c_4 c_3 c_2 c_1. An overflow is said to have occurred if

options.text: all the carry bits c_4 c_3 c_2 c_1 are 1

D. R1 = 1001 and R2 = 1111

B. A - B
C. B - A
D. 2 * B

sourceQuestion:
Let f(x, y, z) = x'y'z' + x'yz' + xy'z' + xyz' be a switching function. Which one of the following is valid?
A. yz' is a prime implicant of f
B. xy is a minterm of f
C. xz' is an implicant of f
D. y is a prime implicant of f
sourceAnswer: C. xz' is an implicant of f

parsed.stem: Let f(x, y, z) = x'y'z' + x'yz' + xy'z' + xyz' be a switching function. Which one of the following is valid?

options.text: yz' is a prime implicant of f
xy is a minterm of f
xz' is an implicant of f
y is a prime implicant of f

sourceQuestion:
The below figure shows four D-type flip-flops connected as a shift register using a XOR gate. The initial state and three subsequent states for three clock pulses are also given.

State table:
| State               | Q_A | Q_B | Q_C | Q_D |
|---------------------|-----|-----|-----|-----|
| Initial             |  0  |  1  |  1  |  1  |
| After the first clock  |  0  |  1  |  1  |  1  |
| After the second clock |  0  |  0  |  1  |  1  |
| After the third clock  |  0  |  0  |  0  |  1  |

The state Q_A Q_B Q_C Q_D after the fourth clock pulse is

A. 0000
B. 1111
C. 1001
D. 1000

| After the first clock  |  0  |  1  |  1  |  1  |
| After the second clock |  0  |  0  |  1  |  1  |
| After the third clock  |  0  |  0  |  0  |  1  |

sourceQuestion:
Consider a Boolean function f(w, x, y, z). Suppose that exactly one of its inputs is allowed to change at a time. If the function happens to be true for two input vectors i_1 = (w_1, x_1, y_1, z_1) and i_2 = (w_2, x_2, y_2, z_2), we would like the function to remain true as the input changes from i_1 to i_2 (i_1 and i_2 differ in exactly one bit position) without becoming false momentarily. Let f(w, x, y, z) = sum(7, 11, 12, 13, 15). Which of the following cube covers of f will ensure that the required property is satisfied?

A. wyz, wxy'z, xyz, xy'z, wxz
B. wyz, wxy'z, xyz
C. wyz, wxy'z, wxz
D. wxy'z, xyz, xy'z, wxz
sourceAnswer: A. wyz, wxy'z, xyz, xy'z, wxz

parsed.stem: Consider a Boolean function f(w, x, y, z). Suppose that exactly one of its inputs is allowed to change at a time. If the function happens to be true for two input vectors i_1 = (w_1, x_1, y_1, z_1) and i_2 = (w_2, x_2, y_2, z_2), we would like the function to remain true as the input changes from i_1 to i_2 (i_1 and i_2 differ in exactly one bit position) without becoming false momentarily. Let f(w, x, y, z) = sum(7, 11, 12, 13, 15). Which of the following cube covers of f will ensure that the required property is satisfied?
